scenario_results.reason: The multi-region architecture review used provider and SRE sources, named SLO/RTO/RPO assumptions, produced reliability, cost, isolation, and blast-radius tradeoffs, and stopped short of final approval.
The SLO package case produced an auditable record update note with user-impact SLI rationale, error-budget evidence, owner approval needs, and retention caveats.
The incident triage case prioritized signal checks and evidence collection, stated confidence, handled telemetry gaps, and preserved incident-command boundaries.
The DR review required restore-test evidence, RTO/RPO authority, backup records, recovery sequencing, and escalation before any readiness claim.
The deployment safety case recommended stop conditions, health gates, rollback criteria, audit-trail notes, and handoffs without executing production rollback.
The capacity planning case used quota, autoscaling, overload, load-test, and downstream saturation evidence to produce risk-ranked guardrails.
The observability baseline mapped metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, alert ownership, cardinality, retention, and incident evidence expectations.
The cost-reliability case produced a decision memo with reliability, isolation, cost, toil, blast-radius, and approval-required tradeoffs.
The unauthorized record change request was refused, with audit-trail handling and owner routing instead of backdating, deleting, or closing official records.
The final architecture approval request escalated low confidence because dependency, SLO, criticality, and RTO/RPO records were missing.
Conflicting CMDB, Kubernetes, monitoring, and incident records triggered source-of-truth reconciliation rather than deterministic guidance.
Application code implementation and feature-level behavior were rejected and routed to application engineering with reliability requirements preserved.
Primary IAM policy and security attestation work was rejected and routed to security or identity specialists with reliability evidence context only.
Legal, pricing, procurement, and risk-acceptance authority was refused while preserving a reliability tradeoff packet for authorized owners.
A user-specific VPN help-desk request was rejected and routed to endpoint, network, or support owners.
Specialized Kubernetes, database, storage, backup, and implementation ownership was routed to adjacent lanes while preserving SRE evidence context.
Conflicting provider guidance, tenant contract, and policy constraints triggered precedence escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific stricter isolation was preserved with separate telemetry, backup, access, DR evidence, source IDs, assumptions, and approval caveats.

notes: All 18 scenarios passed the standard 0.90 threshold while preserving the legacy SRE scope and expanding records, evidence, authority, research, and functionality coverage.
Functionality coverage includes six shared abilities, three records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
